explanation: This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the current state is TRANSITION and the pilot is in control (standby), then the next state will be STANDBY.

The LTL property states that it is always the case that if the autopilot state is TRANSITION, then eventually within a certain duration (N_DURATION), the autopilot state will be STANDBY, provided the pilot is in control (standby).  This directly captures the requirement that the autopilot must transition to STANDBY from TRANSITION when the pilot takes control.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the autopilot is in the TRANSITION state, then eventually it will transition to the STANDBY state when the pilot is in control. The property uses the LTL template G (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3))) with the boolean expressions defined as follows: bool_exp2 is state_is_TRANSITION, bool_exp3 is state_is_STANDBY & standby, and N_DURATION is set to 1.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is in the TRANSITION state, then if the pilot is in control (standby), the system will eventually transition to STANDBY. The formula uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure this holds true at all times. The implication makes sure that the change of state from TRANSITION to STANDBY is conditional on standby.

The LTL property states that globally, if the state is TRANSITION, then the autopilot will eventually transition to STANDBY if the pilot is in control (standby) or the autopilot will always be in STANDBY if the pilot is in control. The property ensures that when the autopilot is in transition and the pilot takes control, the autopilot will transition to standby.

We use the template 12 which uses the 'G' operator to express that this condition should always hold and uses the 'V' operator which expresses that the transition to the STANDBY state or remaining in the STANDBY state occurs at some point in time.

The `G` (Globally) operator ensures this holds true for all time, and the `F` (Eventually) operator combined with the implication (`->`) and the condition `standby` ensures the correct behavior during standby.

N_DURATION is a placeholder for a time bound, though one is not explicitly mentioned in the requirements.
